app.footer: DocuSort · running on your NAS · v{version}
update.installing: Update is being installed — this can take 1–2 minutes…
update.installed_manual: Update installed — the service has to be restarted manually:
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of your documents and their cost.
library.title: Library
library.subtitle_default: Search all processed documents by category, year or full text.
library.subtitle_review: Documents with low confidence — for manual review.
upload.title: Upload documents
upload.subtitle: PDF or image — by drag & drop, file picker, folder picker or directly from a phone scan. Classification runs live — you see here when a document is done.
upload.drop_label_hint: PDF, JPG, PNG, TIFF · multiple files possible
upload.folder_picker_hint: Including sub-folders — all supported files are uploaded and classified
upload.ocr_hint: OCR + Claude take 15–60 s per PDF depending on page count. The watcher waits 5 s after the last byte before it starts — see live status above.
stage.needs_repick: Please re-select — the tab was closed, the file is no longer in browser memory
stage.queued: In queue · waiting for stable size
upload.background_hint: Uploading in the background — you can close this tab.
upload.foreground_hint: Uploading — keep this tab open until the bar finishes. Already-uploaded documents continue to be classified on the server even if you close the tab.
retry.heading: Retry classification
retry.hint: Re-sends the stored OCR text to Claude and refiles the document. No extra OCR cost.
trash.title: Trash
trash.subtitle: Documents you've deleted. Restore them or purge them permanently.
trash.hint_active: The document is moved to _Trash/ but keeps its metadata. You can restore it later.
trash.hint_in_trash: Restore moves the file back to the library. Purge deletes it permanently.
trash.confirm_purge: Permanently delete this document? This cannot be undone.
trash.confirm_empty: Permanently delete all documents in the trash? This cannot be undone.
sync.heading: Backup & Cloud sync
sync.subtitle: Export the library as a single ZIP, or sync it to your cloud via rclone (iCloud, Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, S3 …).
sync.disabled_hint: Cloud sync is disabled. Set sync.enabled: true and a sync.remote in your config.yaml to enable it.
sync.rclone_missing: rclone is not installed. On Debian: sudo apt install rclone, then run rclone config once to set up the remote.
setup.title: Welcome to DocuSort
setup.subtitle: A few steps and DocuSort is ready to go.
setup.step_lang.title: Which language?
setup.step_lang.subtitle: The UI and defaults adapt to your choice.
setup.step_ai.title: Choose your AI provider
setup.step_ai.subtitle: DocuSort sends OCR text to an AI provider for classification — cloud or local.
setup.step_ai.base_url_hint: For Ollama: http://localhost:11434/v1 — the server must be reachable and the model loaded.
setup.step_ai.api_key_hint: Stored in secrets.yaml with file mode 0600.
setup.step_ai.model_hint_anthropic: Recommended: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (fast, cheap, with prompt caching).
setup.step_ai.model_hint_compat: Model name as the provider lists it — e.g. llama3.1, qwen2.5, mistral-small.
setup.provider.anthropic_hint: Claude 4 family · cheapest at scale via prompt caching
setup.step_sync.title: Backup & cloud sync (optional)
setup.step_sync.subtitle: Mirror the library to your cloud via rclone — works on a headless box too.
setup.step_sync.no_rclone_hint: Install rclone on the host, then reload this page to access the wizard.
setup.step_sync.oauth_intro: How to get the token (on your laptop, with a browser):
setup.step_sync.oauth_step1: Install rclone (brew install rclone on macOS, otherwise rclone.org/downloads).
setup.step_sync.oauth_step2: Run this command — it opens your browser, you sign in, and rclone prints a JSON token:
setup.step_sync.oauth_step3: Copy the full JSON block and paste it below.
setup.done.title: All set
setup.done.subtitle: DocuSort will restart with your new settings now.
setup.done.hint: As soon as the service is back up, the page redirects to the dashboard.
settings.subtitle: Provider, model, cloud sync and more — all in one place.
settings.ai.heading: AI provider
settings.ai.subtitle: Switch between cloud providers or a local model.
settings.ai.key_keep: Leave blank to keep the stored key
settings.ai.restart_hint: Provider change takes effect after the service restarts.
settings.sync.heading: Backup & cloud sync
settings.sync.subtitle: Manage your rclone remotes — no browser needed on the host.
settings.paths.heading: Storage paths
settings.paths.subtitle: Where DocuSort picks up and files documents.
settings.paths.hint: Paths can be changed in config.yaml — restart required.
setup.step_sync.subtitle_v2: Mirror the library to a local folder / NAS / USB stick (easy), or optionally to the cloud (more setup).
settings.sync.subtitle_v2: Local folder (easy) or cloud storage (rclone, more setup).
settings.sync.local_hint: Mirrors your library via rsync to a path — e.g. a mounted USB drive or network share. No login, no token.
settings.sync.cloud_hint: Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, S3, WebDAV, SFTP. Needs rclone and one-time OAuth for some providers.
settings.sync.local_path_hint: Absolute path — e.g. /mnt/backup/docusort, /media/usb/docusort, or a mounted NAS share. Created if missing.
settings.sync.show_oauth: Show OAuth providers (Drive · Dropbox · OneDrive) — more complex
settings.sync.oauth_warning: These providers need a one-time token dance on your laptop. If you can, use WebDAV/SFTP/S3 instead — much simpler.
